mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples. A 10 basis point rise in FEDFUNDS produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following a 10bp+ FEDFUNDS increase are positive in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession probability spikes. Institutional investors de-risk equity portfolios, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0% from below, or the signal loses statistical significance in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and consumer prices signal demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening. The joint acceleration of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL shows a positive or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, monthly industrial production increases reflect capacity utilization improvements and upward earnings revisions. This supply-side momentum triggers algorithmic buying, yielding positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a directional hit rate below 45% across all identified recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure below 1.1 million units confirms weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity. This demand contraction triggers portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where HOUST drops below 1100.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates labor force participation contraction. This structural labor market shift suppresses aggregate demand growth and triggers negative equity repricing over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease yields a positive or null 60-day forward return IC over rolling 3-year windows, or the divergence metric shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, each quarter point increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future cash flows. This valuation compression directly produces negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms economic expansion and supports corporate revenue forecasts. Trend-following algorithms execute equity purchases, generating positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 2.0% annualized growth are followed by negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for revision noise.
